parse.why: DSC shows no thermally induced phase transitions for HCGPE, indicating it is totally amorphous ("No thermally induced phase transitions can be observed in the DSC curve of HCGPE, indicating that HCGPE is totally amorphous"). No BET surface area or pore volume data, and no acidity (Brønsted/Lewis) values are reported for HCGPE in the provided text.

HCE is a LiFSI/DME liquid electrolyte (LiFSI:DME molar ratio = 1.9; Experimental Section). Reported data for HCE are electrochemical (e.g., activation energy 0.18 eV, t_Li+ = 0.48; Figures S14–S15; LSV shows a lower onset oxidation potential than HCGPE, Figure 1h). The paper provides no morphology/crystal size, no BET/porosity parameters, and no Brønsted/Lewis acidity values for HCE. Thus all schema fields are set to null for HCE.

parse.why: SEM (Fig. 2b) reports: "This LAWTP sample shows an irregular particle morphology with particle sizes ranging from several hundred nanometers to several micrometers." No BET/porosity, acidity, or crystal size values specific to LAWTP are provided.

For PVDF-HFP-SN (0 g LAWTP), the paper reports electrochemical and thermal metrics but no BET/porosity, acidity, crystal size, or Si/Al ratio. Evidence: Fig. 4b gives PVDF-HFP-SN ionic conductivities (1.07×10^-5 and 3.52×10^-5 S/cm at 30 and 50 °C); Table 2 lists its Li-ion transference number tLi+ = 0.296; Fig. 4d shows its DSC melting peak at 134.17 °C with enthalpy 17.81 J/g; Fig. 4e shows an electrochemical stability window of 4.7 V. While Section 2.2 notes the sample is obtained as an “electrolyte membrane,” no explicit morphology (e.g., particle shape) is characterized for PVDF-HFP-SN in R&D/figures. Therefore morphology, crystal size, porosity, and acidity are set to null.

Photographs and SEM of the PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP solid electrolytes show LAWTP particles distributed in the PVDF-HFP matrix with some non-penetrating pores (Fig. 3c–e), which applies to the set of SCEs including the 0.05 g LAWTP sample. No specific numeric BET/porosity, acidity, or crystallite size, nor sample-specific ionic conductivity or other quantitative properties are reported in text/tables for PVDF-HFP-SN-0.05LAWTP.

characterization.morphology: Flexible white electrolyte membrane; LAWTP particles are distributed in the PVDF-HFP polymer matrix with some non-penetrating pores observed.

parse.why: For PVDF-HFP-SN-0.1LAWTP SCEs, the paper states: “Photographs and SEM images of these SCEs are displayed in Fig. 3… the PVDF-HFP electrolyte is a semi-transparent membrane, and adding the LAWTP filler turns the membrane white… the LAWTP particles are distributed in the PVDF-HFP polymer matrix and some non-penetrating pores can be observed. The cross-sectional image shows a uniform thickness of about 120 μm.” These descriptions apply to all PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP membranes, including the 0.1 g sample. The paper defines PVDF-HFP-SN-0.2LAWTP as one of the SCEs but provides specific numerical results (ionic conductivity, LSV stability window, tLi+, DSC) only for PVDF-HFP-SN-0.1LAWTP, and generic/qualitative statements or images for the SCEs as a group. No quantitative property values are reported specifically for the 0.2LAWTP sample (Fig. 4a–b lists numbers only for pure, SN, and 0.1; Table 2 contains only SN and 0.1; Fig. 4c–e and mechanical data focus on 0.1).
